summary: Expose the full GaMMA BGMM config contract up front so executors stop doing serial missing-key reruns.

atomic_operations.rationale: Round 0 wasted two retries because the skill did not force a one-pass audit of the required station columns and config keys before rerun.
atomic_operations.expected_effect: Executors check `oversample_factor` plus `x(km)`/`y(km)`/`z(km)` in one pass after the first GaMMA `KeyError` instead of patching one key per retry.
atomic_operations.risk: A stricter preflight may add a small amount of up-front checklist overhead.

atomic_operations.rationale: The current required-keys table lists `oversample_factor` but does not surface `config["x(km)"]`, `config["y(km)"]`, and `config["z(km)"]`, which caused the second runtime failure.
atomic_operations.expected_effect: The documented config contract now matches the key set the executor needed to reach the first successful BGMM run.
atomic_operations.risk: The table is slightly denser because the config bounds and station-coordinate names now coexist in the same section.

atomic_operations.rationale: The executor eventually succeeded only after assembling a complete BGMM config; the skill lacked a single copyable snippet that included all of those keys together.
atomic_operations.expected_effect: Executors can paste a known-good `use_amplitude=False` BGMM starter config without omitting `oversample_factor` or the `x(km)`/`y(km)`/`z(km)` bounds.
atomic_operations.risk: Users with materially different tuning targets may still need to adjust thresholds after the first successful run.
